extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT outsprinted PIDCOCK and SCHACHMANN from a three-up break that formed when PIDCOCK attacked after KWIATKOWSKI was caught following the final Cauberg.
tactical_patterns: PIDCOCK attacked immediately after KWIATKOWSKI caught, exploiting momentary recovery hesitation in elite group after Cauberg.
Mechanical at final Cauberg base eliminated ROGLIČ, proving equipment reliability crucial on late technical climbs.
VAN AERT bridged from reduced 40-rider group in final kilometer, showing superior positioning over Matthews-led chase.
Three INEOS riders (KWIATKOWSKI, CARAPAZ, PIDCOCK) in elite selection reveals numerical advantage insufficient without right timing.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: bridged from chase group in final kilometer then won sprint, superior positioning negated numbers disadvantage
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: positive: launched winning move immediately after KWIATKOWSKI caught, exceptional timing sense on debut Amstel
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: made final three-up selection but distanced in sprint, positioned well despite no team support
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: solo attack caught but team still placed PIDCOCK in winning move, sacrificial role executed
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: initiated Cauberg attack but faded badly over summit, early aggression backfired in 38-climb format
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: mechanical at final Cauberg base eliminated contention, positioning too far forward for technical section
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: made elite selection and led chase sprint for fourth, strong but lacked acceleration against specialists
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: bridged to elite group aged 40, last rider to make selection shows exceptional tactical awareness
